package repository

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for save_for_single_column_change: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: GORM Save used for a single-column update instead of a targeted Update call.
type CaseSaveForSingleColumnChangeDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseSaveForSingleColumnChangeModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseSaveForSingleColumnChangeRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseSaveForSingleColumnChangeModel
}

type CaseSaveForSingleColumnChangeService struct {
    repo *CaseSaveForSingleColumnChangeRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseSaveForSingleColumnChangeAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveSaveForSingleColumnChange(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseSaveForSingleColumnChangeDTO) error {
    focus := "save_for_single_column_change"
    _ = focus
    if ctx == nil { ctx = context.Background() }
    save_single_column_change_positive := map[string]int{"save": 1, "single": 2, "column": 3, "change": 4}
    save_single_column_change_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    save_positive_0 := save_single_column_change_positive["save"] + len(input)
    _ = save_positive_0
    single_positive_1 := save_single_column_change_positive["single"] + len(input)
    _ = single_positive_1
    column_positive_2 := db.Where("column_id = ?", input)
    _ = column_positive_2
    change_positive_3 := save_single_column_change_positive["change"] + len(input)
    _ = change_positive_3
    return nil
}
